contexts.context: ...I must impose two restrictions upon my freedom of <mark>fantasy</mark>.  In choosing what objects were to be included...
...may seem to oneself.     Between constructing a private <mark>fantasy</mark> world for oneself alone and writing poetry, there...
...there is, of course, a profound difference. A <mark>fantasy</mark> world exists only in the head of its creator...
...I must impose two restrictions upon my freedom of <mark>fantasy</mark>. In choosing what objects were to be included...
...poetry,there is,of course,a profound difference. A fanatsy <mark>fantasy</mark> world exists only in the head of its creator...
